standaard_secties.INTRODUCTION:
Thank you for your valued enquiry. Flame Spray Technologies is pleased to provide this quotation, and we thank you for your interest in our company and our products. After you have completed your review, at your request, FST are willing to come to your facility and explain the offer in detail. Also, FST is willing to work with you to come to the best possible solution for your application needs.

At Flame Spray Technologies, we understand that the investment into a thermal spray system is not only a financial investment, but also an investment into people and know-how and putting trust into the company that will be responsible for the build, maintenance, and support of the system.

We, therefore, would like to invite you to our company to meet the people you might be working with in the future. We want you to meet our customer support people, our service people, our coating and application engineers and all the other people active in our company. You should understand how we handle orders (small or large). You should understand how we build systems, prepare samples, etc.

Flame Spray Technologies is an IS9001:2015 and ISO14001:2015 approved company.

Feel free to contact your local FST representative to make an appointment to visit our offices in The Netherlands, Singapore or Grand Rapids Michigan
standaard_secties.COATING & APPLICATION SUPPORT:
Flame Spray Technologies is offering our services to develop coatings and applications using the system that you purchase. After full testing (and/or after pre-acceptance) at the FST facilities, agreed to coatings can be sprayed and applications can be developed in accordance with agreed to specifications.

This coating/application development can include the robot programming and part fixturing design. By offering this service and transferring the developed technology and know-how to the customer, the system will enter production in a much shorter period of time.

FST will develop coatings to demonstrate the system has the ability to spray the applications the system is developed for and is able to conform to the to be agreed coatings specifications. For all the specified coatings, test results including coating microstructure, tensile bond strength, bend test, and micro- hardness tests can be made per relevant specifications.

If the above is of interest, FST will prepare a detailed and separate offer for the above or below listed services.

Through its global expertise on equipment, processes, coating production and applications, FST can offer remote and on-site support to Customers for the following:

• Process support including coating and production
development and optimization
• Efficiency improvement programs
• Specific training, such as Thermal Spray process
training or dedicated robot programming courses
(FST recommends initial robot training to be done
by robot OEM training center)
• Laboratory support
• Design and manufacturing of tooling, fixtures,
masking, etc.
• Coating application know-how transfer
• Production overflow work
standaard_secties.SERVICE ORGANIZATION:
The basis for a professional service organization is the full understanding of the Customer’s requirement for an efficient and time effective technical service to support the installed equipment. FST has a team of highly qualified and authorized service engineers who provide remote diagnostic support, system trouble-shooting, breakdown visits and preventative maintenance and calibration support.

In the event of a system breakdown or other irregularities, FST will react promptly, if possible within the same day (maximum within 12 hours) with telephone support and (if needed) remote diagnostic support to determine the cause of the problem. If it becomes apparent that the problem can not be solved remotely, FST is committed to promptly dispatch a service engineer.

The FST Installation, Maintenance & Calibration Team is also trained to provide remote support using FST|SmartGlass™. Thanks to the FST|SmartGlass™, the FST expert is able to provide detailed and direct support and instructions. This is made possible by real-time video and audio transmission (Contact FST for more information).

FST also offers maintenance contracts to perform preventative maintenance work and calibration service at agreed intervals. FST recommends conducting annual (or twice a year, depending on system usage) calibration of all process relevant components, such as mass flow controllers, voltage/current displays, flow and pressure sensors, etc.
standaard_secties.REMOTE DIAGNOSTICS:
As a standard, the 50 series has a self-diagnostics system that initiates alarms and/or E-stops in the event of a system failure or when specific process values (gas flows and pressures, cooling water flow, pressure and temperatures, air extraction flow, etc.) are exceeding preset limits. The alarms are displayed on the 50 series Touch Screen and/or on the Service Page.

The remote diagnostic feature has proven to be a very helpful and valuable tool to minimize the time required to analyze system failure and to detect a possible failure cause very early in the process. Generally, it improves the system uptime significantly. By entering the system PLC remotely, FST can detect the system alarm and error status of all system components, helping us to determine the possible problem areas. The detail of detection can vary from blown fuses up to components that are malfunctioning. FST uses secure eWON technology, a secure VPD remote access for PLC’s. (www.ewon.biz)
standaard_secties.MATERIALS AND TECHNOLOGY: Flame Spray Technologies is a Leader in supplying its customers Thermal Spray Powders, including Carbides, Ceramics and Metals. FST is able to offer a complete solution; sarting with the System and Application development followed by the long- term and reliable supply of high quality thermal spray powders. FST’s labaratories in various locations around the world provide customers with an opportunity to even better understand coatings applied for specific applications. This creates a higher added value for the FST customers by providing in-depth information.
standaard_secties.SPARE PARTS AVAILABILITY:
The overall success of a thermal spray coating operation depends on several factors. Reliable and high class thermal spray equipment contributes to the best possible performance. In the same way high quality spare parts and consumables are crucial for consistent coating production and quality.

For all companies that perform thermal spray processes, a reliable source of supply is important. FST offers a wide range of first class spare parts and consumables.

This offering includes but is not limited to: gun parts such as nozzles, electrodes, powder injectors, barrels, air caps and wire guides. And also powder feeder parts such as, powder disks, suction / spreaders, stirrers, injectors and seal packages.

As part of the system documentation, FST will supply an extensive spare part list with recommended quantities. Consumable (wear) parts and standard spare parts are supplied from our stock. Products supplied from stock are available for delivery on a one (1) working day notice basis.

II. EQUIPMENT DELIVERY, INSTALLATION AND TRAINING

Kick-off meeting at Customer After order acceptance, FST will organize a technical “Kick-Off” meeting with the Customer representatives to clarify all technical details and to discuss and present the final project specifications, lay-out drawings, Customer facility requirements, project planning and Customer pre- installation details resulting in final design review which is confirmed by the customer.

On-site installation and system start-up at customer site The final installation and erection - mechanical and electrical - will be performed by representatives of Flame Spray Technologies at the customers’ plant. The costs for travel expenses, local hotel lodging etc. are included in the mentioned machine price. Building construction work, such as hacking, breaking, digging, breakthroughs, demolition is not included. It is expected to take 1 week.

Final acceptance testing at customer’s premises at customer site (SAT)
After the final installation of the machine onsite FST will perform a final-acceptance test. The final
acceptance will:
• Demonstrate functionality of the coating booth readiness for production
• Demonstrate the integration between the robotic manipulation systems and the coating unit
including all safety.
• Validate that torch ignition parameters function correctly for each of the torches and systems
defined.
• Ensure the upgraded booth connects to and streams data to the network as defined in
previous projects.
• Provide calibration report demonstrating intolerance conditions
• Ensure each of the APS torches is defined and selectable by operations.
• Define an “Any torch” APS recipe that allows engineering to access the maximum capability of
power supplies and gas flows

After successful final acceptance a Final-Acceptance Protocol will be undersigned by both parties and the warranty period starts. SAT expected to take 2 days.

Training During the final acceptance period our engineer will instruct your designated maintenance personnel and operators in the operation and maintenance of the equipment. This training will take place at the Purchaser’s premises. A maximum of 4 people will be trained.

Your personnel will be trained in the following fields: - Operation and programming (0.5 day) - Mechanical, Electrical and Pneumatic Maintenance (0.5 day)

technische_blokken.MP-50 LF SYSTEM SET-UP:
With the MP-50/LF system Flame Spray Technologies is offering a State-of-the-Art Single process Mass Flow Controlled HVOF coating system with outstanding performance. The MP-50/LF system is built to be Reliable and Functional, ensuring highest possible coating quality.

Only First-Class Components and the latest available Technology have been used to build the MP-50/LF system. This guarantees the highest possible up-time and safety. The closed-loop Mass Flow Controlled and Touch Screen Operated System, brings a flexible and accurate process control at your fingertips. Through smart design, while not compromising on reliability, quality, safety and performance, the MP-50/LF system is a premium quality HVOF solution.

The MP-50/LF system incorporates a separation of fuel, gas and electrical components built into a modular designed unit. Flow, pressure, and temperature sensors are installed to monitor the process and provide warnings when the system is operating outside of its specified limits.

The MP-50/LF system is an HVOF system to operate and control multiple liquid fuel HVOF Torches including: eGun. If torches other then offered in this offer are to be integrated with the system, FST needs to be informed if any other gun, then supplied with the system will be used. The system design might have to be changed and adjusted to accommodate the correct and safe operation of another torch.

The MP-50/LF system supports most commercially available powder feed units. This flexibility gives the customer the opportunity to select the preferred feeder without compromising the overall system’s quality and/or performance.

The MP-50/LF system uses an operator friendly full color touch screen that allows an easy programming and process visualization during operation. The MP-50/LF system can store up to 1000 recipes in the system’s PLC. The MP-50/LF can be designed to be mobile, allowing the system to be moved from location to location.

The MP-50 Digital Series is designed around modularity by working with separate Gas Modules and Jam-Boxes for each of the processes. Together with the control system, this significantly enhances the safety, stability, and reliability of the entire system. Further process modules can easily be added in the future i.e., APS, HVOF, Combustion
technische_blokken.MAIN FEATURES OF THE MP-50:
• Modular construction for maximum safety
• Closed loop mass flow controllers for all process gases including carrier gas.
• Remote diagnostics for improved maintenance service through eWon
• The process controller is PLC based providing maximum reliability and flexibility.
• A graphical Touch Screen control panel provides clear and user-friendly access to process
control parameters and monitoring functions.
• Data acquisition/trending with storage on USB stick
• Password protected system entry levels
• Full and detailed alarm registration and history
• CE Conformity / UL conformity
technische_blokken.MP-50 SCOPE OF SUPPLY:
The MP-50 HVOF System from Flame Spray Technologies is an advanced and reliable coating system that provides the repeatability and accuracy required in today’s thermal spray production environment.

The MP-50 multi-process consists of the following main components:

• MP-50 Touch Screen Control Panel
• MP-50 Electrical Module
• MP-50 Gas Modules
• MP-50 Jam Boxes
• iPS100 Power Supply
• 2x FST-20MC Powder feeder
• Cable and Hose Packages
technische_blokken.MP-50 TOUCH SCREEN CONTROL CONSOLE:
The Touch Screen control panel provides all programming features and visual display of all set and actual spraying parameters. Different menus can be selected to enable the operator to overview the entire process data. The touch screen control panel (installed on a Swing Arm or Floor Stand).

The MP-50 control system is based on the latest (Siemens) PLC technology. Up to 1000 spray “recipes” can be stored. The Idle mode is a special feature where the process will run with minimum parameters. When idle button is pressed, the parameters will reduce to idle settings. When it is pressed again original settings will be active again saving gas consumptions.

The MP-50 software (FST | Horizon™) has separate control menus for HVOF spraying processes. The operator touch screen is a high end 22inch Siemens PRO touch screen. The latest software makes the system easy to operate, gives a good overview of the parameters and works smoothly. All the process related parameters are displayed on the screen and are controlled by the system.

The software is prepared for future upgrades and the use of torches other than those supplied with the original system. The software has all gun limits stored, which makes sure that each parameter (per torch) can never exceed the maximum allowable limits. This is part of the overall system safeties.

The touch screen contains heavy duty push buttons to start up the man/machine interface control console. Also, a (red) e-stop and a (blue) reset button are present on the module. An USB connection for data logging and receipt back-up. Another black cap protects the RJ45 connection which is used for uploading of possible software upgrades.

The alarm history menu shows all the alarms and failures that come up during the process. All the hardware is designed for operation in heavy duty circumstances (IP65).

Examples of the Horizon operating software menu’s

1.3 REMOTE DIAGNOSTICS, DATA LOGGING, ALARM REGISTRATION Idle Mode The Idle mode is a special feature where, when pressed, the process will run with minimum parameters values. When idle button is pressed again the full operation parameters settings will be active again. Data trending All process parameters can be followed in real-time. The Data Trending Page presents all data in a graphical format for easy monitoring. Data logging In addition to data trending which displays data, the new software utilizes data logging. Data logging records all important process data directly onto an external USB device, which can be processed using a standard computer. This process is fully automatic and does not require operator input. The USB device is connected via a USB slot on side of the touch panel display. New Electrical Module design The Electrical Module has been redesigned according to latest safety and electrical regulations and standards. Further the electrical module has been redesigned with easy maintenance in mind. Improved recipe management FST has gathered input from its customers and system users and modified recipe management accordingly. The system now allows for easy recipe selection, modification, and creation from the main operational page. Recipes back-up to external USB device All the recipes stored in the system can be backed-up to a USB device. By a simple push of a button the recipes are transferred to the USB device. The USB device is simply plugged into the USB slot on the side of the touch screen panel. Re-installing recipes has never been so easy. User levels Different system access levels through Login Passwords: Operator | Quality | Supervisor | Service. Improved alarm page including alarm history and remedy messages. Clear and well documented alarm registration (type of alarm, date, time). Including alarm history registration. Consumption page This page shows the volumes of media used (gasses, fuel). This provides better understanding of total costs involved. Improved remote diagnostics via internet using secure eWON technology. eWon technology allows FST to remotely access the system for diagnostic functions or software up- grades/up-dates. The security of the internet connection is comparable with modern remote banking access systems. Remote diagnostics is only available for FST to monitor. Stop watches with current and last cycle time. Gives the operator a stopwatch function which will also display the last cycle time. This is a useful feature when running production of same parts. Displaying actual cooling capacity or heat losses to cooling water. This information helps operators understand the real-time power level of the system. Service page including actual status of all PLC signals. All data on valves, sensors etc. is displayed on this page and gives engineers a detailed overview of the system status. This feature helps service engineers and technicians to quickly locate an issue thus reducing costly down time. This page is intended for qualified FST service engineers and technicians only.

Individual receipt alarm tolerance levels Per receipt the levels for alarms setting can be set and saved.

Remote Access The MP-50 Control Module has an integrated communication device, called eWon, to enable “remote access” via a secure internet connection. In this way, FST has remote and secure access to the MP-50 PLC for failure analyses, system errors, troubleshooting or software updates. This can save considerable time in system troubleshooting or detecting and correcting problems. The Customer must provide an internet connection to the MP-50 Control Module.

Remark: Remote access is only available for FST. There is no possibility for the end user to monitor the system via remote access.

With the remote access system, FST can monitor on-line all system functions and equipment status (valves, controllers, pressure sensors, etc.) even when the system is in operation or during the start- up phase. The remote access also provides necessary and helpful information in the case of phone support to the operator or maintenance staff to correct a problem, or to narrow down the problem area to prepare the dispatch of a service engineer with the correct replacement parts and tools. The Remote Access system has proven to be an extremely helpful tool for trouble-shooting the system effectively, and to increase the availability and uptime of the system for production. FST has no access to Customer specific information such as spraying parameters.
technische_blokken.DIGITALLY CONNECTED SMART COMPONENTS:
The latest 50 series is fully digitally connected. By means of the eWon the FST service team can zoom into each individual digitally connected component for service and troubleshoot intervention. This hardware also allows for predictive maintenance interventions.

No external influence of the signal: Data transfer is based on a 24V signal. Screened cables and required grounding are no longer necessary.

No measured value losses: The entire measured value transmission is digital. Transmission that is prone to errors and calculation of analogue signals is replaced.

Easy sensor replacement: all sensor parameters are stored in the master and transferred to the replaced unit.

Tamper free: therefore, wrong settings by the operators is no longer possible.

Identification: only like for like replacement. No wrong sensors accepted.

Wire break detection / diagnosis: wire break or short-circuit is immediately detected.
technische_blokken.MP-50 ELECTRICAL MODULE:
The Electrical Module contains the PLC unit and software to control all system modules. All process parameters are closed loop controlled for maximum reliability and repeatability. System status and all alarm and safety features are constantly monitored by the PLC based controller.

Advanced PLC technology with Siemens software ensures constant and repeatable spraying conditions. The PLC can store up to 1000 different spraying recipes. The Control Module includes an integrated 24V DC power source and I/O module for control of all sub-components (Profinet network).

Further, the MP-50 Electrical Module is equipped with I/O connection plugs (Male/Female) so it can also be integrated with peripheral equipment or auxiliary components, such as powder feeders, heat exchangers, dust collectors (integration details to be agreed prior project start).

Additional features: Automatic LED lights inside electrical module for service purpose.
technische_blokken.HVOF LIQUID / GAS FUEL MODULE:
The HVOF Gas Control Module contains the Mass Flow Controllers for all the process gases including the carrier gas. For safety reasons the gas module is continuously vented, preventing the build-up of gases and/or fumes inside the gas module in case of a leak. To assure the highest possible quality all gas lines and fittings are manufactured from stainless steel. The Gas Module also holds a compressed air line for component cooling. Cooling air can be switched on/off via the Touch Screen Controller or via the robot program. The system will give a visual alarm if a process is operating outside its set limits. The fuel and the oxygen are closed loop controlled via highly accurate Mass Flow Controllers (MFC). All set and actual values are displayed on the Touch Screen Panel.

The HVOF Gas Module contains all MFC’s, pressure sensors, ethanol supply pump and safety items to control the following process gases/fuels:

One (1) Oxygen MFC 22 - 1100 nlpm

One (1) Liquid fuel MFC 0 - 38 lph

One (1) Hydrogen MFC 22 - 1100 nlpm

The accuracy of the mass flow controllers is 2% Full Scale.

Each gas line is equipped with a pressure gauge on the supply side. For safety reasons, the Gas Module cabinet is equipped with a gas sensor. Furthermore, all gas lines and fittings are manufactured from stainless steel. Gas inlet pressures are constantly monitored, and the system will give a visual alarm if the gas pressures are too low. An air cool switch block (on/off) for the cooling stand is included which can be used for cooling the parts during the process.
technische_blokken.APS GAS MODULE:
The APS Gas Control Module contains the Mass Flow Controllers for all the process gases including carrier gas. For safety reasons the gas module is continuously vented, preventing the build-up of gases and/or fumes inside the gas module in case of a leak. To ensure the highest possible quality all gas lines and fittings are manufactured from stainless steel. The Gas Module also holds a compressed air line for component cooling. Cooling air can be switched on/off via the Touch Screen Controller or via the robot program. The system will give a visual alarm if a process is operating outside its set limits. The process gases are closed loop controlled via highly accurate Mass Flow Controllers (MFC). All set and actual values are displayed on the Touch Screen Panel

The APS Gas Module contains all MFC’s, pressure sensors and safety items to control the following process gases:

One (1) Argon MFC 1 - 100 nlpm

One (1) Hydrogen MFC 1 - 20 nlpm

One (1) Nitrogen MFC 1 - 100 nlpm

The accuracy of the mass flow controllers is 2% Full Scale

Each gas line is equipped with a pressure gauge on the supply side. For safety reasons, the Gas Module cabinet is equipped with a gas sensor. Furthermore, all gas lines and fittings are manufactured from stainless steel. Gas inlet pressures are constantly monitored, and the system will give a visual alarm if the gas pressures are too low.
technische_blokken.JAM BOX MODULE:
• Water distribution for three processes
• Ignition devices for APS and HVOF
• Choke to protect rectifier
• Safety functions for APS and HVOF
(cooling water flow/temperatures)
• AIR purge system for HVOF
• Automatic process selection
technische_blokken.PLASMA POWER SUPPLY iPS-100:
PLY iPS-100 The secondary chopped Power Supply (Elmatech), in combination with the MP-50 System, offers the possibility of operating different plasma torches at a high and constant quality level. The secondary chopped technology guarantees stable current and homogeneous and constant plasma over the entire power range, even at very low power levels.

Highly efficient and heavy-duty secondary chopped (inverter) rectifier with the following characteristics:

• Cascaded gun ready – Capable of Sinplex + extended stack
• 1000Amp/100Volt or 500Amp/200V
• Duty cycle 100%
• Electricity Supply in accordance with “EMC” standards
• HF Choke
• Voltage at Gun

The secondary chopped power supply represents the latest technology for plasma rectifiers. This technology provides substantial advantages compared to thyristor-based plasma rectifiers, such as unsurpassed plasma current stability (over the entire power range), ripple-free currents avoiding plasma power fluctuations, and high electrical efficiency,

200V – 500A

100V –1000A
technische_blokken.CABLES AND HOSES PACKAGE:
The system will be supplied with all required cables and hoses. The approx. cable/hose lengths are:

- From Drop-off point to Gas Modules 10m - Torch to Feeder 10m

Gas supply lines ending with appropriate regulators, shut-off valves and threaded fittings should be provided by the Customer to a drop point on a wall near the booth. FST will supply and install lines and hoses from the appropriate modules to the drop points.
technische_blokken.ELECTRICAL DISTRIBUTION CABINET: A separate electrical distribution cabinet (EDU) is used to distribute all electrical power to all system sub-components. The cabinet is equipped with all adequate electrical fuses and main switches. With this distribution cabinet only one main connection of the electricity supply from the customer is required. This unit is supplied when a Turnkey option is purchased or MP-50 + handling or booth/filter. It is not required when an MP-50 is purchased on its own.
technische_blokken.AIR DISTRIBUTION UNIT: An air-cooling distribution unit including proportional valve (pressure regulated via Horizon operation software) for the torch air jet, component cooling jets and manual air gun.
technische_blokken.SYSTEM SAFETIES:
The following safety items will be installed and integrated with the MP-50 system Control System. Any signal resulting from the below mentioned sensors or items will generate an alarm or E-stop procedure.

• E-Stop button on Touch Screen Module.
• Valves in the Gas/Fuel Module that close automatically if an alarm is generated.
• Supply gas pressures: in case of low pressures, the system generates an alarm/E-stop.
• In case of low water flow, high water temperature, the system generates an alarm/E-stop.
• A flow sensor will be supplied to be installed inside the exhaust ducting. The sensor will detect
if there is actual flow through the ducting before the MP-50 starts. If there is no flow, the system
will not start.
technische_blokken.INTEGRATION OF AUXILIARY COMPONENTS:
The MP-50 system can control the following external components via fixed I/O integration such as:

• Heat Exchanger Cooling Water Pump start/stop
• Dust Collector start/stop
• Powder Feeder master / slave
• Cooling Air on/off (Standard)

The above possibilities are only a list of auxiliary components that can be integrated with the MP-50 system other, and more components can be integrated with the system, but must be agreed upon before the system is ordered.
technische_blokken.SYSTEM CALIBRATION: After system installation the system will be fully calibrated by a qualified FST engineer using OEM certified calibration equipment. After calibration of the system the calibration certificates will be handed over to the responsible person at customer. After calibration the system will be ready for production.
technische_blokken.SIGNALIZATION STACK LIGHT:
A signalization light is connected to the MP-50 system. Green, orange, and red will show various states of the MP-50 system including:

• Hard and soft alarms
• System spray cycle in process
• System e-stop engaged
• System ready

2. POWDER FEEDER
technische_blokken.Two (2) FST-10/MC POWDER FEEDER:
The FST10/MC single powder feeder is one of the easiest and most reliable powder feeders in its class; it has low maintenance requirements and supplies powder to the gun in a manner that ensures high quality coatings day in/day out. The powder feeder that can be used with all powder based thermal spray processes including plasma and HVOF. Its proven design has become the standard in the thermal spray industry. The MFC controlled feeder offers unparalleled powder feeding accuracy and repeatability.

The feeder operates in a volumetric principle that directly controls the powder feed rate by a variable speed powder wheel. Using Profinet interfaces all data of the feeder (set/actual values) can be controlled by master device (e.g., robot control). and transferred back to the feeder PLC.

• One (1) MFC Nitrogen/Argon 1-45 nlpm
• Double powder feeder based on the volumetric principle used for
accurate and homogeneous powder feeding.
• Powder Feeder is supplied with
o 2x 1.5-liter hopper HP,
o 2x 5 liter hopper (Al version),
• Hoppers (Al Version only) supplied with Heater Jacket
• The powder feeder is remotely controlled by the MP-50.
• The entire powder feeder is designed to meet all required safety and gas control standards.

POWDER FEEDER FST-10/MC TECHNICAL FEATURES
• RPM of feeder wheel: 0,2 to 10 in 0,1 steps (Set/actual value)
• RPM of stirrer in percentage 0-100% in 1% steps (Set/actual value)
• Complete powder hopper mounted on a mobile cabinet:
• Controller: Siemens
• 2 pcs. motor driven hopper with stirrer with WIRE ca. 1.5 Liter Volume with:
• Standard disc groove 16 mm x 1,2 mm; Feed rate: approx. 5 to 200 g/min
(other type of disc on request)
• 2 pcs. Electric driven stirrer with WIRE (0-100%)
technische_blokken.POWDER FEEDER HOPPER STATION (optional):
onal) When multiple hoppers are in operation, storing the not used hopper is always and issue. To solve this FST developed at Powder Hopper Station where hoppers can be securely stored, and heater blankets kept in operation heating the powder. This will allow for (overnight) storage without the risk of powder getting moist because it cools down. The powder hopper required can be pickup and put in production without having to worry that the has become moist again while stored.

1.17 ACCURASPRAY 4.0 - GO / NO-GO (OPTIONAL)

The Accuraspray 4.0 is a sensitive Go-No Go sensor that will trigger alarms as soon as the spray plume properties step out of the pre-determined acceptance range. This minimizes the risk of spraying bad coatings on expensive parts. Ensure consistent coatings by continuously monitoring the properties of the sprayed material, either in manual or automatic mode. Now your operators can react and readjust before the process falls out of its green window and significantly improve your process Cpks & Ppks.

Technical Specifications

Measurement ranges
• Particle temperature range
o 1000°C and higher at 3% accuracy
o 1832°F and higher at 3% accuracy
• Particle velocity range
o 5 - 1200 m/s at 2% accuracy
o 16.3 - 3900 ft/s at 2% accuracy
• Spray plume intensity & peak height
o 2% accuracy
• Spray plume width & position mm accuracy
o 0.004 in. accuracy
Measurement volume information
• CCD camera field of view
o 400 mm
o 15.7 in.
• Accuraspray measurement volume
o 3.2 mm DIA x 25 mm DOF = 200mm3
o in. DIA x 1 in. DOF = 0.01 in3
Integrated with HMI

1.19 FST | HORIZON™ MAINTENANCE MODULE (OPTIONAL) The FST|Horizon maintenance scheduler software is used to plan and schedule maintenance and calibration intervals. In the user-friendly scheduler, standard and/or personal intervals can be entered / scheduled.

Avoids unplanned down-time and guarantee optimal system performance with FST Horizon™ maintenance scheduler.

3. THERMAL SPRAY TORCHES

Torches not included in scope of supply

4. SAFETY FEATURES INCLUDED IN MP-50

The following safety items will be installed and integrated with the MP-50 Control System. Any signal resulting from below mentioned sensors or items will generate an alarm or system-stop procedure.

• Shut-off valves for the combustible gases, included in the MP-50 Gas Modules that close
automatically when a gas leak is detected.

• Monitoring and safeguarding of the gas supply pressure; in case of low pressures, the system
generates an alarm.

• The MP-50 has a self-diagnostic system that
continuously monitors the status of the system
and its peripheral components and safety sensors.

• After the process starts command on the MP-50
Touch Screen all starting criteria are accepted (for
example: gas pressures and flows, air extraction,
cooling water flow and temperatures, gas sensors
and doors switches, and others), the system will
declare the OK status.

• A flow sensor will be supplied to be installed inside the exhaust ducting. The sensor will detect
if there is actual flow through the ducting before the MP-50 starts. If there is no flow, the system
will not start.
